name: Kairos Pharma, LTD.
business: Kairos Pharma is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing therapeutics for cancer patients targeting immune suppression and drug resistance. Its portfolio includes seven drug candidates: five pre-clinical or clinical-stage drugs (KROS series) and two therapeutic agents (ENV 105 and ENV 205) for prostate, lung, breast, and brain cancers. The company has not yet received FDA approval for any product.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company advancing therapeutics for cancer patients that are designed to overcome key hurdles in immune suppression and drug resistance.
Our Enviro Therapeutics, Inc. subsidiary will strive to co-develop companion biomarkers with all drugs in its portfolio, enabling identification of potential drug responders prior to therapy.

catalysts.event: Phase 1 trial for non-small cell lung cancer started
catalysts.sentence: As a result, we began a Phase 1 trial for non-small cell lung cancer in September 2023

catalysts.event: Phase 2 trial for prostate cancer started
catalysts.sentence: in September 2023 opened a randomized Phase 2 trial for prostate cancer.

catalysts.event: NIH grant awarded for ENV105 companion biomarker development
catalysts.sentence: On May 21, 2024, the NIH announced that it was awarding Neil Bhowmick, PhD, our Chief Scientific Officer and also a Cedars-Sinai Professor of Medicine, a grant of $3.2 million to support the development of the mechanism of action and companion biomarkers in research that is being performed by Cedars-Sinai in conjunction with our ongoing Phase 2 trial for ENV105 (carotuximab) and apalutamide treating castrate resistant prostate cancer patients.

tamClaims.claim: Global prostate cancer therapeutics market $7.9 billion
tamClaims.sentence: The global prostate cancer therapeutics market size was valued at USD 7.9 billion and at USD 1.7 billion for EGFR mutant non-small cell lung cancer.

tamClaims.claim: Potential US$9 billion gross sales for ENV105
tamClaims.sentence: There are some 300,000 castrate resistant prostate cancer patients in the U.S. eligible to be given combination therapy of ENV 105 + androgen signaling inhibitors for $5,000 per month (based on comparable neutralizing antibodies). This would suggest a potential US$9 billion gross sales for ENV 105 for a six-month dose in the U.S. for this single indication.
Global immunotherapy market was valued at $226 billion by 2024
